tasks.title: Install Development Dependencies
tasks.description: Add openai, vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8, and @playwright/test packages to the project with proper dev/prod separation.
tasks.details:
Install the required packages using pnpm:

```bash
pnpm add openai@^6.31.0
pnpm add -D vitest@^4.1.0 @vitest/coverage-v8@^4.1.0 @playwright/test@^1.58.0
```

Update package.json scripts:
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "test": "vitest",
    "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui"
  }
}
```

The openai package v6.31.0 provides TypeScript-first support with proper type definitions. Vitest 4.1.0 requires Vite >= 6.0.0 and Node.js >= 20.0.0 (project uses Node >= 22.0.0, Vite 7.0.0). Playwright 1.58.x supports Node.js 20.x, 22.x, 24.x.

After installation, run `pnpx playwright install` to download browser binaries for Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify installation by running `pnpm test --version` and `pnpm exec playwright --version`. Confirm all packages appear in package.json with correct version ranges. Run `pnpm install` and ensure no dependency conflicts occur.

tasks.title: Configure Vitest
tasks.description: Set up Vitest configuration with coverage provider and test patterns for the React Router project.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm test` to verify Vitest starts successfully. Create a simple test file `app/lib/__test__.ts` with `it('works', () => expect(true).toBe(true))` to confirm the setup. Remove the test file after verification.

tasks.title: Configure Playwright
tasks.description: Set up Playwright configuration for E2E testing with Chromium browser targeting the development server.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm test:e2e --ui` to verify Playwright opens correctly. Create a minimal `e2e/smoke.spec.ts` test that navigates to '/' and asserts the page loads. Delete after verification.

tasks.title: Create .env.example with OPENAI_API_KEY
tasks.description: Update .env.example to include the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable documentation.
tasks.details:
Update the existing `.env.example` file to include the OpenAI API key:

```env
# Sentry DSN for error tracking
VITE_SENTRY_DSN=

# OpenAI API key for AI furigana generation (server-only — no VITE_ prefix)
# Get your API key from: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
```

The OPENAI_API_KEY does NOT use the VITE_ prefix because:
1. It must remain server-only (never bundled into client code)
2. React Router SSR actions access it via `process.env['OPENAI_API_KEY']`
3. Vite only exposes VITE_-prefixed vars to client code

Verify `.gitignore` already includes `.env` (confirmed in analysis). The `.env.example` is safe to commit as it contains no actual secrets.

Update `env.d.ts` if needed to add the type declaration (though not required for server-only vars).
tasks.testStrategy: Verify .env.example exists and contains OPENAI_API_KEY placeholder. Confirm .env is in .gitignore. Create a local .env with a valid API key for integration testing.

tasks.title: Define FuriganaToken Types
tasks.description: Create the FuriganaToken schema and type definitions used by the parser and ReadingView components.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm test app/schema/furigana.test.ts --run`, `pnpm type-check`, and `pnpm exec eslint app/schema/furigana.ts app/schema/furigana.test.ts`. Confirm schema parse success/failure and type guards via unit tests.

tasks.title: Implement Annotation String Parser
tasks.description: Create the parseAnnotationString function that transforms AI annotation output into a typed FuriganaToken array.
tasks.testStrategy: Create comprehensive unit tests in `app/lib/furigana/parser.test.ts` covering all edge cases from the PRD: single kanji, multiple consecutive ruby tokens, pure hiragana, pure romaji, empty string, unclosed braces, nested braces, empty reading braces. Target 100% line coverage.

tasks.title: Write Parser Unit Tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for parseAnnotationString covering all edge cases defined in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm test:coverage` and verify 100% line coverage for parser.ts. All 14 test cases must pass. Review coverage report in coverage/index.html.

tasks.title: Create OpenAI Client Module
tasks.description: Create a server-only module that exports a pre-configured OpenAI SDK client instance.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: Mock the openaiClient in route action tests. Unit test: Verify the module throws when OPENAI_API_KEY is unset by temporarily removing it from test environment.

tasks.title: Create System Prompt Module
tasks.description: Create the system prompt and user message builder for GPT-4o-mini furigana generation.
tasks.details:
Create `app/lib/ai/prompts.ts`:

```typescript
// app/lib/ai/prompts.ts
// Server-only — prompt configuration for furigana generation

/**
 * System prompt for GPT-4o-mini furigana annotation.
 * Includes few-shot examples to lock in the expected output format.
 */
export const FURIGANA_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a Japanese language assistant that adds furigana readings to kanji.

Your task:
1. Annotate every kanji compound with its hiragana reading in the format: 漢字{よみ}
2. Return ONLY the annotated string - no explanations, no markdown, no quotes
3. Non-kanji characters (hiragana, katakana, punctuation, numbers, latin) pass through unchanged

Examples:

Input: 日本語を勉強しています。
Output: 日本語{にほんご}を勉強{べんきょう}しています。

Input: 東京に行きました。
Output: 東京{とうきょう}に行{い}きました。

Input: こんにちは！元気ですか？
Output: こんにちは！元気{げんき}ですか？

Input: 今日は2024年1月1日です。
Output: 今日{きょう}は2024年{ねん}1月{がつ}1日{にち}です。`;

/**
 * Builds the user message for the furigana generation request.
 * @param text - The Japanese text to annotate
 */
export function buildUserMessage(text: string): string {
  return text;
}
```

Key design decisions:
- System prompt is a const (immutable, testable via snapshot)
- Few-shot examples demonstrate exact format expected
- Examples cover: compound kanji, consecutive kanji, pure hiragana, mixed content
- `buildUserMessage` is simple now but allows future preprocessing (e.g., text normalization)
- Model: GPT-4o-mini (specified in action, not prompt)
tasks.testStrategy: Create `app/lib/ai/prompts.test.ts` with snapshot test for FURIGANA_SYSTEM_PROMPT. Test buildUserMessage returns input unchanged. Run `pnpm test app/lib/ai/prompts.test.ts`.

tasks.title: Implement Route Action for Furigana Generation
tasks.description: Create the server action in home.tsx that calls GPT-4o-mini, validates responses, and returns structured data.
tasks.testStrategy: Create `app/routes/home.test.ts` with mocked openaiClient. Test cases: valid response returns tokens, API error returns error with originalText, empty text returns error, over-limit text returns error.

tasks.title: Create InputArea Component
tasks.description: Build the controlled textarea component with character counter, validation, and keyboard shortcuts.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual testing: verify character counter updates, verify keyboard shortcut submits form, verify disabled state prevents interaction. E2E test will cover full integration.

tasks.title: Create ReadingView Component
tasks.description: Build the component that renders FuriganaToken array as semantic ruby HTML elements.
tasks.testStrategy: Visual testing: render component with sample tokens, verify ruby text appears above kanji. Verify pure-text input renders without ruby elements. Check browser DevTools for correct HTML structure.

tasks.title: Add Ruby CSS Base Rules
tasks.description: Add CSS rules to app.css ensuring ruby, rt, and rp elements render correctly under Tailwind v4's reset.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm dev`, create test page with <ruby>漢字<rt>かんじ</rt></ruby>, verify furigana appears above kanji text in browser. Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari.

tasks.title: Wire Home Route with Form and Conditional Rendering
tasks.description: Complete the home.tsx route component with form handling, loading states, error display, and conditional InputArea/ReadingView rendering.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual testing: paste text, submit, verify loading state, verify ReadingView displays. Test error path by disconnecting network. Verify Cmd+Enter shortcut works.

tasks.title: Write E2E Tests with Playwright
tasks.description: Create Playwright E2E tests covering the primary furigana generation flow, character limit validation, and empty submission handling.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm test:e2e` and verify all 5 tests pass. Run with `--headed` flag for visual debugging. Check HTML report in playwright-report/ for failures.
